codeReview.summary: Memory blocks feature fully implemented with Project/User/Task block types, auto-loading via MemoryBlockLoader trait, and context assembly via ContextAssembler. 7 tests pass covering block creation, loading, and context assembly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker attempted to invoke 'rust-worker' skill but it does not exist. features.json specifies skillName: 'Rust' but worker transcript shows initial attempt was for 'rust-worker'. The AGENTS.md documents 'Rust' skill exists in .factory/skills/ but the mission worker base skill references 'rust-worker' which doesn't exist. This is a documentation/implementation mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Skill "rust-worker" not found' error, then worker correctly used 'Rust' skill instead. skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false with deviation noting rust-worker skill doesn't exist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill documentation is thorough but focuses on language patterns. For workers implementing features in existing crates (like swell-memory), there may be value in documenting crate-integration patterns - how to properly expose new modules, use pub use from swell_core, integrate with existing store patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker spent time exploring crate structure (swell-memory/src/lib.rs, swell-core/src/traits.rs) to understand how to add blocks module and what types to import from swell_core.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The code has a minor redundancy: DefaultBlockLoader has both #[derive(Default)] and an explicit new() method that does the same thing. While not incorrect (new() is useful for clarity), AGENTS.md doesn't document when to use derive(Default) vs a custom new() constructor.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/blocks.rs:42 - impl DefaultBlockLoader { pub fn new() -> Self { Self } } while #[derive(Default)] is already on line 41

summary: Implementation review passed. All expected behaviors (Project/User/Task blocks, auto-loading, context assembly) implemented correctly. 7 tests pass. Minor observations about skill name mismatch (rust-worker vs Rust) documented for shared state improvement.
